name: Close
one_liner: Close is a sales-focused CRM with built-in calling, email, and SMS that helps SMB sales teams manage leads, contacts, opportunities, and outreach workflows.
primary_auth: OAuth2 (authorization code + refresh token) for user-facing integrations; API key over HTTP Basic (key as username, blank password) for scripts/server-side
access_notes: Close is a paid CRM with no free plan, but a 14-day free trial (no credit card) gives full product access; API keys are generated self-serve in Settings > Developer > API Keys, and OAuth apps can be created from the developer settings.
api_notes: REST API v1 at api.close.com/api/v1 covering leads, contacts, opportunities, activities (calls/emails/SMS/meetings/notes), tasks, custom fields/objects, sequences, smart views, bulk actions, users, reporting, event log, and webhooks; the official (experimental) OpenAPI spec lists 156 paths / ~297 operations. Rate limits with 429 + backoff guidance.
mcp_notes: Vendor ships an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.close.com/mcp (HTTP Streamable) with OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration and scoped permissions (mcp.read, mcp.write_safe, mcp.write_destructive); documented at help.close.com/docs/mcp-server and listed as a Claude connector. Community servers (e.g. close-crm-cli) also exist.

evidence.claim: Close API supports API keys (HTTP Basic, key as username with blank password), created self-serve under Settings > Developer > API Keys
OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow with refresh tokens (offline_access scope), 1-hour access tokens, and token revocation endpoint

REST API v1 (base https://api.close.com/api/v1) documents leads, contacts, opportunities, activities, custom fields, webhooks, event log and more; experimental OpenAPI spec at api.close.com/api/openapi.json shows 156 paths / 297 operations
Close is paid-only with a 14-day free trial (no credit card, full product access) — the trial is the self-serve path to working credentials
Official Close MCP server at mcp.close.com/mcp with OAuth 2.0 DCR and read/write_safe/write_destructive scopes

open_questions: Did not explicitly confirm whether OAuth app registration requires any review/approval (appears self-serve from developer settings, but the docs excerpt did not state it outright); exact rate-limit thresholds and whether they vary by plan were not detailed.
